channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mini Crossword and Superquiz Puzzles for March 16
item.description: The Age presents its Superquiz for Monday, March 16, 2026, offering readers an engaging way to test their knowledge and enjoy a quick mental challenge. The article highlights the availability of a Min...

item.title: Pakistan to Export Surplus Food to Gulf Countries While Safeguarding Domestic Supply
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has instructed the concerned authorities to devise a strategy for exporting surplus food items to Gulf countries, making sure that Pakistan's domestic food needs are not ...

item.title: Ramesh Chennithala Confirms UDF Unity and Readiness Ahead of Kerala Assembly Polls
item.description: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala stated on Sunday that the United Democratic Front (UDF) is fully united and prepared to contest the upcoming Kerala Assembly elections. Speaking in Guruvayur,...

item.title: UK Government Prepares for Energy Bill Intervention Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Shadow Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has indicated that the UK government is prepared to intervene on energy bills and fuel duty if the ongoing conflict in Iran leads to a significant surge in prices. ...

item.title: TikTok Explains How E Dey Handle Content, Safety, and Government Requests in Nigeria
item.description: Tokunbo Ibrahim, wey be TikTok Head of Government Relations and Public Policy for Sub-Saharan Africa, don yarn how TikTok dey manage their operations for Nigeria and other African markets, especially ...

item.title: Fragment of Iranian Missile Strikes Residential Building Used by US Consul in Israel
item.description: According to reports from Israeli media on Sunday, March 15, 2026, a fragment from an Iranian missile struck a residential building that is used by the United States consul in Israel. This incident ha...

item.title: NDLEA intercept cocaine and codeine in major operations across Abuja and Lagos
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don arrest one 74-year-old man, Ikwuakalom Nwakoro Emeka, for the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja, as he try carry 11 kilograms of cocaine ...

item.title: L1 Capital raises significant funds for new gold-focused investment company with founders contributing $100 million
item.description: L1 Capital’s founders, Mark Landau and Raphael Lamm, are personally investing at least $100 million into their new gold-focused listed investment company (LIC), signalling strong confidence in the ven...

item.title: Jair Bolsonaro remains in ICU with pneumonia while serving prison sentence
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, aged 70, is currently in an intensive care unit in Brasilia due to pneumonia, although his kidney function has shown improvement. Bolsonaro, who governed Bra...

item.title: House of Representatives targets approval of four key LEDAC bills before March 21 adjournment
item.description: The House of Representatives is set to approve four priority bills under the Legislative-Executive Development Advisory Council (LEDAC) Common Legislative Agenda before Congress adjourns on March 21. ...

item.title: Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine leaves country after contesting election results
item.description: Uganda's opposition leader, Bobi Wine, whose real name is Robert Kyagulanyi, has temporarily left the country following a presidential election in January that kept long-time President Yoweri Museveni...

item.title: iCAUR to Launch V23 and 03T Electric Models in South Africa This May
item.description: iCAUR, a new electric vehicle brand under the Chery Group, is set to officially launch in South Africa this May, bringing two fully electric models: the V23 and the 03T. The V23 will serve as the entr...

item.title: US President Trump escalates Iran conflict, urges allies to secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump don talk say dem fit do more strikes on Iran's Kharg Island, wey na important oil export hub. E still push say countries wey dey benefit from oil pass through the Strait of H...

item.title: Indian-flagged tanker 'Jag Laadki' departs safely after UAE port attack
item.description: An Indian-flagged crude oil tanker, Jag Laadki, narrowly avoided danger when the Fujairah port in the UAE was attacked on 14 March 2026 while the vessel was loading crude. The tanker, carrying approxi...

item.title: Pakistani security forces neutralise five terrorists in Lakki Marwat operation
item.description: On March 15, 2026, Pakistan's security forces carried out a well-coordinated operation in Lakki Marwat district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, targeting terrorists reportedly backed by India, according to a s...

item.title: Filipino and Korean Stars Collaborate in Reality Cooking Show in Seoul
item.description: A new reality cooking show called 'Kumusta' is bridging Filipino and Korean cultures through food. The show features Filipino actors Jodi Sta. Maria, Janella Salvador, Francine Diaz, and Arci Muñoz wo...

item.title: Beckhams and other celebrities share Mother's Day tributes amid ongoing family tensions with Brooklyn
item.description: On Mother's Day in the UK, David and Victoria Beckham posted heartfelt tributes to Victoria, praising her as an inspiration to their four children and expressing gratitude for their family. David shar...

item.title: How a Sacco Election Candidate Turned to a Quick Loan to Fund His Campaign
item.description: This article narrates the story of a Sacco member who believes that taking a quick loan is the only practical way to run a competitive election campaign within his co‑operative society. It explains th...

item.title: National Lok Adalat disposes over 4,400 cases in Dakshina Kannada and Udupi, pendency remains above 90,000
item.description: After the National Lok Adalat concluded on Saturday, the pending cases in Dakshina Kannada's 46 courts reduced to 54,742, while in Udupi district's 22 courts, it stood at 35,866, keeping the combined ...

item.title: Al-Quds Day protesters and counter-protesters assemble on opposite sides of the Thames in London
item.description: Thousands of protesters and counter-protesters gathered on either side of the River Thames in central London for the annual al-Quds Day demonstration. Police had anticipated a large turnout and deploy...
